news.title: U.S. says it targeted Iranian forces after attacks that killed two American service members
news.summary: Centcom says it hit Iran's coastal surveillance and air defense facilities a day after two U.S. troops were killed in an Iranian strike on Jordan.

news.title: American Airlines CEO lays out his vision to close a more than $3 billion profit gap
news.summary: The carrier is working on improving reliability, investing in more premium seats and lounges. The carrier is considering Boeing and Airbus for a new wide-body plane order.

news.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
news.summary: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say. Analysts say it makes the industry more vulnerable to attacks.

news.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
news.summary: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

news.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
news.summary: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players. It has also formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

news.title: I racked up $35,000 in credit-card debt. Should I file for bankruptcy?
news.summary: “I’m also considering working with a credit-counseling agency or enrolling in a hardship program,” she said.

news.title: Can the ‘Magnificent Seven’ save a stock market that might be doomed without them?
news.summary: Apple, Amazon, Nvidia and peers are quietly coming back to life, and that could easily jolt this sleepy stock market back into gear.

news.title: ‘I am at a loss’: My brother is dying. We are not close. What responsibility do I have to help him?
news.summary: “He is now no longer able to think clearly or communicate his wishes,” his family says. “He has been left with no choice but to die,’ the family says in a statement.

news.title: My husband and I are retired. Our financial adviser, who is in his 30s, called us ‘you guyses.’ Is that unprofessional?
news.summary: “I’m wondering whether I should ask to work with someone closer to our generation,” he says. “I'm wondering whether we should ask for someone from our generation.”

news.title: ‘Respect is important’: If my financial adviser said, ‘Hey,’ my wife and I would walk out. Where do you draw the line?
news.summary: “I met with a financial representative who was not aware that AT&T was the shortened form of American Telephone and Telegraph,” she said.
